notifyUser.description: When enabled, sends brief compaction notices to the user when compaction starts and when it completes (for example, '🧹 Compacting context...' and '🧹 Compaction complete'). Disabled by default to keep compaction silent and non-intrusive.

compaction.title: Compaction
compaction.description: Compaction tuning for when context nears token limits, including history share, reserve headroom, and pre-compaction memory flush behavior. Use this when long-running sessions need stable continuity under tight context windows.

base.title: Run Retries Base
base.description: Base number of run retry iterations for the embedded OpenClaw runner's outer run loop (default: 24).

perProfile.title: Run Retries Per Profile
perProfile.description: Additional run retry iterations granted per fallback profile candidate (default: 8).

min.title: Run Retries Minimum
min.description: Minimum absolute limit for run retry iterations (default: 32).

max.title: Run Retries Maximum
max.description: Maximum absolute limit for run retry iterations to prevent runaway execution (default: 160).

runRetries.title: Run Retries
runRetries.description: Outer run loop retry iteration boundaries for the embedded OpenClaw runner to prevent infinite execution loops during failure recovery.

projectSettingsPolicy.title: Embedded OpenClaw Project Settings Policy
projectSettingsPolicy.description: How embedded OpenClaw handles workspace-local `.openclaw/settings.json`: "sanitize" (default) strips shellPath/shellCommandPrefix, "ignore" disables project settings entirely, and "trusted" applies project settings as-is.

executionContract.title: Embedded OpenClaw Execution Contract
executionContract.description: Embedded OpenClaw execution contract: "default" keeps the standard runner behavior, while "strict-agentic" keeps OpenAI/OpenAI Codex GPT-5-family runs acting until they hit a real blocker instead of stopping at plans or filler.

embeddedAgent.title: Embedded OpenClaw
embeddedAgent.description: Embedded OpenClaw runner hardening controls for how workspace-local agent settings are trusted and applied in OpenClaw sessions.

mode.title: Human Delay Mode
mode.description: Delay style for block replies ("off", "natural", "custom").

minMs.title: Human Delay Min (ms)
minMs.description: Minimum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 800).

maxMs.title: Human Delay Max (ms)
maxMs.description: Maximum delay in ms for custom humanDelay (default: 2500).

imageMaxDimensionPx.title: Image Max Dimension (px)
imageMaxDimensionPx.description: Max image side length in pixels when sanitizing transcript/tool-result image payloads (default: 1200).

imageQuality.title: Image Quality
imageQuality.description: Image-tool media compression preference: "auto" adapts to provider/model limits and image count, "efficient" saves tokens and bytes, "balanced" keeps the current middle ground, and "high" preserves more detail for screenshots and document images.

directPolicy.title: Heartbeat Direct Policy
directPolicy.description: Controls whether heartbeat delivery may target direct/DM chats: "allow" (default) permits DM delivery and "block" suppresses direct-target sends.

includeSystemPromptSection.title: Heartbeat Include System Prompt Section
includeSystemPromptSection.description: Includes the default agent's ## Heartbeats system prompt section when true. Turn this off to keep heartbeat runtime behavior while omitting the heartbeat prompt instructions from the agent system prompt.

suppressToolErrorWarnings.title: Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
suppressToolErrorWarnings.description: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

timeoutSeconds.title: Heartbeat Timeout (Seconds)
timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time in seconds allowed for a heartbeat agent turn before it is aborted. Leave unset to use agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds when set, otherwise the heartbeat cadence capped at 600 seconds.

skipWhenBusy.title: Heartbeat Skip When Busy
skipWhenBusy.description: When true, defer heartbeat turns on this agent's extra busy lanes: its own session-keyed subagent or nested command work. Cron lanes always defer heartbeat turns.

delegationMode.title: Sub-agent Delegation Mode
delegationMode.description: Prompt-only sub-agent delegation strength. "suggest" keeps the default guidance; "prefer" strongly instructs the main agent to delegate anything more involved than a direct reply via sessions_spawn.

maxSpawnDepth.description: Maximum nesting depth for sub-agent spawning. 1 = no nesting (default), 2 = sub-agents can spawn sub-sub-agents.

maxChildrenPerAgent.description: Maximum number of active children a single agent session can spawn (default: 5).

gpus.title: Sandbox Docker GPUs
gpus.description: Optional Docker GPU passthrough value passed to --gpus, for example "all" or "device=GPU-uuid". Requires a compatible host runtime such as NVIDIA Container Toolkit.

dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.title: Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.description: DANGEROUS break-glass override that allows sandbox Docker network mode container:<id>. This joins another container namespace and weakens sandbox isolation.

network.title: Sandbox Browser Network
network.description: Docker network for sandbox browser containers (default: openclaw-sandbox-browser). Avoid bridge if you need stricter isolation.

cdpSourceRange.title: Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
cdpSourceRange.description: Optional CIDR allowlist for container-edge CDP ingress (for example 172.21.0.1/32).

defaults.title: Agent Defaults
defaults.description: Shared default settings inherited by agents unless overridden per entry in agents.list. Use defaults to enforce consistent baseline behavior and reduce duplicated per-agent configuration.

id.title: Agent Model Runtime ID
id.description: Per-agent model runtime id: "openclaw", "auto", a registered plugin harness id such as "codex", or a supported CLI backend alias such as "claude-cli".

agentRuntime.title: Agent Model Runtime
agentRuntime.description: Optional per-model runtime policy for this agent. Use this for agent-specific model exceptions instead of setting a whole-agent runtime.

models.title: Agent Model Overrides
models.description: Per-agent model catalog overrides keyed by full provider/model IDs.

thinkingDefault.title: Agent Thinking Default
thinkingDefault.description: Optional per-agent default thinking level. Overrides agents.defaults.thinkingDefault for this agent when no per-message or session override is set.

reasoningDefault.title: Agent Reasoning Default
reasoningDefault.description: Optional per-agent default reasoning visibility (on|off|stream). Applies when no per-message or session reasoning override is set.

fastModeDefault.title: Agent Fast Mode Default
fastModeDefault.description: Optional per-agent default for fast mode. Applies when no per-message or session fast-mode override is set.

contextInjection.title: Agent Context Injection
contextInjection.description: Per-agent override for when workspace bootstrap files are injected into this agent's system prompt. Omit to inherit agents.defaults.contextInjection.

bootstrapMaxChars.title: Agent Bootstrap Max Chars
bootstrapMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for max characters of each workspace bootstrap file injected into this agent's system prompt. Omit to inherit agents.defaults.bootstrapMaxChars.

bootstrapTotalMaxChars.title: Agent Bootstrap Total Max Chars
bootstrapTotalMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for max total characters across all workspace bootstrap files injected into this agent's system prompt. Omit to inherit agents.defaults.bootstrapTotalMaxChars.

localModelLean.title: Agent Lean Local Model Mode
localModelLean.description: Per-agent override for lean local-model mode. Enable it for one smaller local-model agent without trimming tools from every agent.

experimental.title: Agent Experimental Flags
experimental.description: Per-agent experimental flags. Omitted fields inherit agents.defaults.experimental.

skills.title: Agent Skill Filter
skills.description: Optional allowlist of skills for this agent. If omitted, the agent inherits agents.defaults.skills when set; otherwise skills stay unrestricted. Set [] for no skills. An explicit list fully replaces inherited defaults instead of merging with them.

maxSkillsPromptChars.title: Agent Skills Prompt Max Chars
maxSkillsPromptChars.description: Per-agent override for the skills prompt character budget. This extends the existing skills.limits.maxSkillsPromptChars path instead of routing the same budget through contextLimits.

skillsLimits.title: Agent Skills Limits
skillsLimits.description: Optional per-agent overrides for skills subsystem budgets. Use this when an agent needs a different skills prompt budget without introducing a second generic context-limits path.

memoryGetMaxChars.title: Agent memory_get Max Chars
memoryGetMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for the default memory_get max character budget.

memoryGetDefaultLines.title: Agent memory_get Line Window
memoryGetDefaultLines.description: Per-agent override for the default memory_get line window when lines is omitted.

toolResultMaxChars.title: Agent Tool Result Max Chars
toolResultMaxChars.description: Per-agent advanced ceiling for the live tool-result max character budget. Omit to inherit defaults or the model-context auto cap.

postCompactionMaxChars.title: Agent Post-compaction Max Chars
postCompactionMaxChars.description: Per-agent override for the post-compaction AGENTS.md excerpt budget.

contextLimits.title: Agent Context Limits
contextLimits.description: Optional per-agent overrides for the focused context budget knobs. Omitted fields inherit agents.defaults.contextLimits.

directPolicy.title: Heartbeat Direct Policy
directPolicy.description: Per-agent override for heartbeat direct/DM delivery policy; use "block" for agents that should only send heartbeat alerts to non-DM destinations.

includeSystemPromptSection.title: Heartbeat Include System Prompt Section
includeSystemPromptSection.description: Per-agent override for whether the default agent's ## Heartbeats system prompt section is injected. Use false to keep heartbeat runtime behavior but omit the heartbeat prompt instructions from that agent's system prompt.

suppressToolErrorWarnings.title: Heartbeat Suppress Tool Error Warnings
suppressToolErrorWarnings.description: Suppress tool error warning payloads during heartbeat runs.

timeoutSeconds.title: Heartbeat Timeout (Seconds)
timeoutSeconds.description: Per-agent maximum time in seconds allowed for a heartbeat agent turn before it is aborted. Leave unset to inherit the merged heartbeat timeout, then agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds when set, otherwise the heartbeat cadence capped at 600 seconds.

skipWhenBusy.title: Heartbeat Skip When Busy
skipWhenBusy.description: Per-agent override that defers heartbeat turns on that agent's extra busy lanes: its own session-keyed subagent or nested command work. Cron lanes always defer heartbeat turns.

avatar.title: Identity Avatar
avatar.description: Agent avatar (workspace-relative path, http(s) URL, or data URI).

delegationMode.title: Sub-agent Delegation Mode
delegationMode.description: Per-agent override for sub-agent delegation strength. Use this for coordinator agents that should stay responsive and push non-trivial work into spawned sub-agents.

base.title: Agent Run Retries Base
base.description: Base number of run retry iterations for this agent.

perProfile.title: Agent Run Retries Per Profile
perProfile.description: Additional run retry iterations granted per fallback profile candidate for this agent.

min.title: Agent Run Retries Minimum
min.description: Minimum absolute limit for run retry iterations for this agent.

max.title: Agent Run Retries Maximum
max.description: Maximum absolute limit for run retry iterations for this agent.

runRetries.title: Agent Run Retries
runRetries.description: Optional per-agent override for the embedded OpenClaw runner's outer run loop retry iteration boundaries.

executionContract.title: Agent Embedded OpenClaw Execution Contract
executionContract.description: Optional per-agent embedded OpenClaw execution contract override. Set "strict-agentic" to keep that agent acting through plan-only turns on OpenAI/OpenAI Codex GPT-5-family runs, or "default" to inherit the standard runner behavior.

embeddedAgent.title: Agent Embedded OpenClaw
embeddedAgent.description: Optional per-agent embedded OpenClaw overrides. Use this to opt specific agents into stricter GPT-5 execution behavior without changing the global default.

gpus.title: Agent Sandbox Docker GPUs
gpus.description: Per-agent Docker GPU passthrough override for sandbox containers.

dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.title: Agent Sandbox Docker Allow Container Namespace Join
dangerouslyAllowContainerNamespaceJoin.description: Per-agent DANGEROUS override for container namespace joins in sandbox Docker network mode.

network.title: Agent Sandbox Browser Network
network.description: Per-agent override for sandbox browser Docker network.

cdpSourceRange.title: Agent Sandbox Browser CDP Source Port Range
cdpSourceRange.description: Per-agent override for CDP source CIDR allowlist.

profile.title: Agent Tool Profile
profile.description: Per-agent override for tool profile selection when one agent needs a different capability baseline. Use this sparingly so policy differences across agents stay intentional and reviewable.

alsoAllow.title: Agent Tool Allowlist Additions
alsoAllow.description: Per-agent additive allowlist for tools on top of global and profile policy. Keep narrow to avoid accidental privilege expansion on specialized agents.

byProvider.title: Agent Tool Policy by Provider
byProvider.description: Per-agent provider-specific tool policy overrides for channel-scoped capability control. Use this when a single agent needs tighter restrictions on one provider than others.

codeMode.title: Agent Code Mode
codeMode.description: Per-agent code mode override. Use this to test or roll out exec/wait tool-surface mode for one agent without enabling it fleet-wide.

allowWithinProvider.title: Agent Cross-Context Messaging (Same Provider)
allowWithinProvider.description: Per-agent message guard for sending to other conversations on the same provider. Set false for current-conversation-only public agents.

allowAcrossProviders.title: Agent Cross-Context Messaging (Across Providers)
allowAcrossProviders.description: Per-agent message guard for sending across providers. Keep false for public or sandboxed agents.

allow.title: Agent Message Action Allowlist
allow.description: Per-agent message action allowlist for the message tool. Set to a minimal list such as ["send"] for public sandbox agents so read, edit, delete, reaction, and other provider-specific message actions stay hidden and blocked.

type.title: Agent Runtime Type
type.description: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).

type.title: Agent Runtime Type
type.description: Runtime type for this agent: "embedded" (default OpenClaw runtime) or "acp" (ACP harness defaults).

agent.title: Agent ACP Harness Agent
agent.description: Optional ACP harness agent id to use for this OpenClaw agent (for example codex, claude, cursor, gemini, openclaw).

backend.title: Agent ACP Backend
backend.description: Optional ACP backend override for this agent's ACP sessions (falls back to global acp.backend).

mode.title: Agent ACP Mode
mode.description: Optional ACP session mode default for this agent (persistent or oneshot).

cwd.title: Agent ACP Working Directory
cwd.description: Optional default working directory for this agent's ACP sessions.

acp.title: Agent ACP Runtime
acp.description: ACP runtime defaults for this agent when runtime.type=acp. Binding-level ACP overrides still take precedence per conversation.

runtime.title: Agent Runtime
runtime.description: Optional runtime descriptor for this agent. Use embedded for default OpenClaw execution or acp for external ACP harness defaults.

list.title: Agent List
list.description: Explicit list of configured agents with IDs and optional overrides for model, tools, identity, and workspace. Keep IDs stable over time so bindings, approvals, and session routing remain deterministic.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Agent runtime configuration root covering defaults and explicit agent entries used for routing and execution context. Keep this section explicit so model/tool behavior stays predictable across multi-agent workflows.

profile.title: Tool Profile
profile.description: Global tool profile name used to select a predefined tool policy baseline before applying allow/deny overrides. Use this for consistent environment posture across agents and keep profile names stable.

allow.title: Tool Allowlist
allow.description: Absolute tool allowlist that replaces profile-derived defaults for strict environments. Use this only when you intentionally run a tightly curated subset of tool capabilities.

alsoAllow.title: Tool Allowlist Additions
alsoAllow.description: Extra tool allowlist entries merged on top of the selected tool profile and default policy. Keep this list small and explicit so audits can quickly identify intentional policy exceptions.

deny.title: Tool Denylist
deny.description: Global tool denylist that blocks listed tools even when profile or provider rules would allow them. Use deny rules for emergency lockouts and long-term defense-in-depth.

byProvider.title: Tool Policy by Provider
byProvider.description: Per-provider tool allow/deny overrides keyed by channel/provider ID to tailor capabilities by surface. Use this when one provider needs stricter controls than global tool policy.

enabled.title: Enable Web Search Tool
enabled.description: Enable managed web_search and optional Codex-native search for eligible models.

provider.title: Web Search Provider
provider.description: Search provider id. Auto-detected from available API keys if omitted.

timeoutSeconds.title: Web Search Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for web_search requests.

cacheTtlMinutes.title: Web Search Cache TTL (min)
cacheTtlMinutes.description: Cache TTL in minutes for web_search results.

enabled.title: Enable Native Codex Web Search
enabled.description: Enable native Codex web search for Codex-capable models.

mode.title: Codex Web Search Mode
mode.description: Native Codex web search mode: "cached" (default) or "live".

allowedDomains.title: Codex Allowed Domains
allowedDomains.description: Optional domain allowlist passed to the native Codex web_search tool.

contextSize.title: Codex Search Context Size
contextSize.description: Native Codex search context size hint: "low", "medium", or "high".

country.title: Codex User Country
country.description: Approximate country sent to native Codex web search.

region.title: Codex User Region
region.description: Approximate region/state sent to native Codex web search.

city.title: Codex User City
city.description: Approximate city sent to native Codex web search.

timezone.title: Codex User Timezone
timezone.description: Approximate timezone sent to native Codex web search.

enabled.title: Enable Web Fetch Tool
enabled.description: Enable the web_fetch tool (lightweight HTTP fetch).

maxCharsCap.title: Web Fetch Hard Max Chars
maxCharsCap.description: Hard cap for web_fetch maxChars (applies to config and tool calls).

timeoutSeconds.title: Web Fetch Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for web_fetch requests.

cacheTtlMinutes.title: Web Fetch Cache TTL (min)
cacheTtlMinutes.description: Cache TTL in minutes for web_fetch results.

userAgent.title: Web Fetch User-Agent
userAgent.description: Override User-Agent header for web_fetch requests.

readability.title: Web Fetch Readability Extraction
readability.description: Use Readability to extract main content from HTML (fallbacks to basic HTML cleanup).

useTrustedEnvProxy.title: Web Fetch Trusted Env Proxy
useTrustedEnvProxy.description: Route web_fetch through a trusted HTTP(S) env proxy and let the proxy resolve DNS. Enable only when that proxy is operator-controlled and enforces outbound policy after DNS resolution.

allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange.title: Web Fetch Allow RFC 2544 Benchmark Range
allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange.description: Allow RFC 2544 benchmark-range IPs (198.18.0.0/15) for fake-IP proxy compatibility such as Clash or Surge.

allowIpv6UniqueLocalRange.title: Web Fetch Allow IPv6 Unique Local Range
allowIpv6UniqueLocalRange.description: Allow IPv6 Unique Local Addresses (fc00::/7) for trusted fake-IP proxy compatibility such as sing-box, Clash, or Surge.

ssrfPolicy.title: Web Fetch SSRF Policy
ssrfPolicy.description: Scoped SSRF policy overrides for web_fetch. Keep this narrow and opt in only for known local-network proxy environments.

web.title: Web Tools
web.description: Web-tool policy grouping for search/fetch providers, limits, and fallback behavior tuning. Keep enabled settings aligned with API key availability and outbound networking policy.

models.title: Media Understanding Shared Models
models.description: Shared fallback model list used by media understanding tools when modality-specific model lists are not set. Keep this aligned with available multimodal providers to avoid runtime fallback churn.

concurrency.title: Media Understanding Concurrency
concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent media understanding operations per turn across image, audio, and video tasks. Lower this in resource-constrained deployments to prevent CPU/network saturation.

directSend.title: Async Media Completion Direct Send
directSend.description: Deprecated compatibility flag. Async media generation completions are requester-session mediated so the agent can decide how to tell the user and use the message tool when source delivery requires it.

enabled.title: Enable Image Understanding
enabled.description: Enable image understanding so attached or referenced images can be interpreted into textual context. Disable if you need text-only operation or want to avoid image-processing cost.

scope.title: Image Understanding Scope
scope.description: Scope selector for when image understanding is attempted (for example only explicit requests versus broader auto-detection). Keep narrow scope in busy channels to control token and API spend.

maxBytes.title: Image Understanding Max Bytes
maxBytes.description: Maximum accepted image payload size in bytes before the item is skipped or truncated by policy. Keep limits realistic for your provider caps and infrastructure bandwidth.

maxChars.title: Image Understanding Max Chars
maxChars.description: Maximum characters returned from image understanding output after model response normalization. Use tighter limits to reduce prompt bloat and larger limits for detail-heavy OCR tasks.

prompt.title: Image Understanding Prompt
prompt.description: Instruction template used for image understanding requests to shape extraction style and detail level. Keep prompts deterministic so outputs stay consistent across turns and channels.

timeoutSeconds.title: Image Understanding Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for each image understanding request before it is aborted. Increase for high-resolution analysis and lower it for latency-sensitive operator workflows.

attachments.title: Image Understanding Attachment Policy
attachments.description: Attachment handling policy for image inputs, including which message attachments qualify for image analysis. Use restrictive settings in untrusted channels to reduce unexpected processing.

models.title: Image Understanding Models
models.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for image understanding when you want to override shared media models. Put the most reliable multimodal model first to reduce fallback attempts.

enabled.title: Enable Audio Understanding
enabled.description: Enable audio understanding so voice notes or audio clips can be transcribed/summarized for agent context. Disable when audio ingestion is outside policy or unnecessary for your workflows.

scope.title: Audio Understanding Scope
scope.description: Scope selector for when audio understanding runs across inbound messages and attachments. Keep focused scopes in high-volume channels to reduce cost and avoid accidental transcription.

maxBytes.title: Audio Understanding Max Bytes
maxBytes.description: Maximum accepted audio payload size in bytes before processing is rejected or clipped by policy. Set this based on expected recording length and upstream provider limits.

maxChars.title: Audio Understanding Max Chars
maxChars.description: Maximum characters retained from audio understanding output to prevent oversized transcript injection. Increase for long-form dictation, or lower to keep conversational turns compact.

prompt.title: Audio Understanding Prompt
prompt.description: Instruction template guiding audio understanding output style, such as concise summary versus near-verbatim transcript. Keep wording consistent so downstream automations can rely on output format.

timeoutSeconds.title: Audio Understanding Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for audio understanding execution before the operation is cancelled. Use longer timeouts for long recordings and tighter ones for interactive chat responsiveness.

language.title: Audio Understanding Language
language.description: Preferred language hint for audio understanding/transcription when provider support is available. Set this to improve recognition accuracy for known primary languages.

headers.title: Audio Request Headers
headers.description: Additional HTTP headers merged into audio provider requests after provider defaults. Use this for tenant routing or proxy integration headers, and keep secrets in env-backed values.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

token.title: Audio Request Bearer Token
token.description: Bearer token used when audio request auth.mode is authorization-bearer. Keep this in secret storage rather than inline config.

mode.title: Audio Request Auth Mode
mode.description: Auth override mode for audio requests: "provider-default" keeps the normal provider auth, "authorization-bearer" forces an Authorization bearer token, and "header" sends a custom header/value pair.

headerName.title: Audio Request Auth Header Name
headerName.description: Header name used when audio request auth.mode is header. Match the exact upstream expectation, such as x-api-key or authorization.

value.title: Audio Request Auth Header Value
value.description: Header value used when audio request auth.mode is header. Keep secrets in env-backed values and avoid duplicating provider-default auth unnecessarily.

prefix.title: Audio Request Auth Header Prefix
prefix.description: Optional prefix prepended to the custom auth header value, such as Bearer. Leave unset when the upstream expects the raw credential only.

auth.title: Audio Request Auth Override
auth.description: Optional auth override for audio provider requests. Use this when the upstream expects a non-default bearer token or custom auth header shape.

mode.title: Audio Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy mode for audio requests: "env-proxy" uses environment proxy settings, while "explicit-proxy" uses the configured proxy URL only for this request path.

tls.title: Audio Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: TLS settings applied when connecting to the configured audio proxy, such as custom CA trust for an internal proxy gateway.

mode.title: Audio Request Proxy Mode
mode.description: Proxy mode for audio requests: "env-proxy" uses environment proxy settings, while "explicit-proxy" uses the configured proxy URL only for this request path.

url.title: Audio Request Proxy URL
url.description: Explicit proxy URL for audio provider traffic when proxy.mode is explicit-proxy. Keep credentials out of inline URLs when possible and prefer secret-backed env injection.

tls.title: Audio Request Proxy TLS
tls.description: TLS settings applied when connecting to the configured audio proxy, such as custom CA trust for an internal proxy gateway.

proxy.title: Audio Request Proxy
proxy.description: Proxy transport override for audio requests. Use env-proxy to respect process proxy settings, or explicit-proxy to force a dedicated proxy URL for this provider path.

tls.title: Audio Request TLS
tls.description: Direct TLS client settings for audio provider requests, including custom CA trust, client certs, or SNI overrides for managed gateways and internal endpoints.

request.title: Audio Request Overrides
request.description: Low-level HTTP request overrides for audio providers, including custom headers, auth, proxy routing, and TLS client settings. Use this for proxy-backed or self-hosted transcription endpoints when plain baseUrl/apiKey fields are not enough.

attachments.title: Audio Understanding Attachment Policy
attachments.description: Attachment policy for audio inputs indicating which uploaded files are eligible for audio processing. Keep restrictive defaults in mixed-content channels to avoid unintended audio workloads.

models.title: Audio Understanding Models
models.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for audio understanding, used before shared media model fallback. Choose models optimized for transcription quality in your primary language/domain.

echoTranscript.title: Echo Transcript to Chat
echoTranscript.description: Echo the audio transcript back to the originating chat before agent processing. When enabled, users immediately see what was heard from their voice note, helping them verify transcription accuracy before the agent acts on it. Default: false.

echoFormat.title: Transcript Echo Format
echoFormat.description: Format string for the echoed transcript message. Use `{transcript}` as a placeholder for the transcribed text. Default: '📝 "{transcript}"'.

enabled.title: Enable Video Understanding
enabled.description: Enable video understanding so clips can be summarized into text for downstream reasoning and responses. Disable when processing video is out of policy or too expensive for your deployment.

scope.title: Video Understanding Scope
scope.description: Scope selector controlling when video understanding is attempted across incoming events. Narrow scope in noisy channels, and broaden only where video interpretation is core to workflow.

maxBytes.title: Video Understanding Max Bytes
maxBytes.description: Maximum accepted video payload size in bytes before policy rejection or trimming occurs. Tune this to provider and infrastructure limits to avoid repeated timeout/failure loops.

maxChars.title: Video Understanding Max Chars
maxChars.description: Maximum characters retained from video understanding output to control prompt growth. Raise for dense scene descriptions and lower when concise summaries are preferred.

prompt.title: Video Understanding Prompt
prompt.description: Instruction template for video understanding describing desired summary granularity and focus areas. Keep this stable so output quality remains predictable across model/provider fallbacks.

timeoutSeconds.title: Video Understanding Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for each video understanding request before cancellation. Use conservative values in interactive channels and longer values for offline or batch-heavy processing.

attachments.title: Video Understanding Attachment Policy
attachments.description: Attachment eligibility policy for video analysis, defining which message files can trigger video processing. Keep this explicit in shared channels to prevent accidental large media workloads.

models.title: Video Understanding Models
models.description: Ordered model preferences specifically for video understanding before shared media fallback applies. Prioritize models with strong multimodal video support to minimize degraded summaries.

enabled.title: Enable Link Understanding
enabled.description: Enable automatic link understanding pre-processing so URLs can be summarized before agent reasoning. Keep enabled for richer context, and disable when strict minimal processing is required.

scope.title: Link Understanding Scope
scope.description: Controls when link understanding runs relative to conversation context and message type. Keep scope conservative to avoid unnecessary fetches on messages where links are not actionable.

maxLinks.title: Link Understanding Max Links
maxLinks.description: Maximum number of links expanded per turn during link understanding. Use lower values to control latency/cost in chatty threads and higher values when multi-link context is critical.

timeoutSeconds.title: Link Understanding Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Per-link understanding timeout budget in seconds before unresolved links are skipped. Keep this bounded to avoid long stalls when external sites are slow or unreachable.

models.title: Link Understanding Models
models.description: Preferred model list for link understanding tasks, evaluated in order as fallbacks when supported. Use lightweight models first for routine summarization and heavier models only when needed.

visibility.title: Session Tools Visibility
visibility.description: Controls which sessions can be targeted by sessions_list/sessions_history/sessions_send. ("tree" default = current session + spawned subagent sessions; "self" = only current; "agent" = any session in the current agent id; "all" = any session; cross-agent still requires tools.agentToAgent).

enabled.title: Tool-loop Detection
enabled.description: Enable repetitive tool-call loop detection and backoff safety checks (default: false).

historySize.title: Tool-loop History Size
historySize.description: Tool history window size for loop detection (default: 30).

warningThreshold.title: Tool-loop Warning Threshold
warningThreshold.description: Warning threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 10).

unknownToolThreshold.title: Unknown-tool Loop Threshold
unknownToolThreshold.description: Block repeated calls to the same unavailable tool after this many misses (default: 10).

criticalThreshold.title: Tool-loop Critical Threshold
criticalThreshold.description: Critical threshold for repetitive patterns when detector is enabled (default: 20).

genericRepeat.title: Tool-loop Generic Repeat Detection
genericRepeat.description: Enable generic repeated same-tool/same-params loop detection (default: true).

knownPollNoProgress.title: Tool-loop Poll No-Progress Detection
knownPollNoProgress.description: Enable known poll tool no-progress loop detection (default: true).

windowSize.title: Post-compaction Loop Guard Window Size
windowSize.description: Number of post-compaction attempts during which the guard stays armed (default: 3). Lower values are stricter; higher values give the agent more attempts before abort.

enabled.title: Enable Tool Search
enabled.description: Enables Tool Search. When on, OpenClaw hides large tool catalogs behind `tool_search_code` or structured search/describe/call tools during embedded runtime runs.

mode.title: Tool Search Surface
mode.description: Choose the model-facing surface: "code" exposes `tool_search_code`; "tools" exposes structured search/describe/call fallback tools.

codeTimeoutMs.title: Tool Search Code Timeout
codeTimeoutMs.description: Maximum milliseconds for one `tool_search_code` execution. Runtime clamps values to the supported 1s..60s range.

searchDefaultLimit.title: Tool Search Default Results
searchDefaultLimit.description: Default number of Tool Search results returned when the model omits a limit. Runtime clamps this to `maxSearchLimit`.

maxSearchLimit.title: Tool Search Max Results
maxSearchLimit.description: Maximum number of Tool Search results a model can request. Runtime clamps values to the supported 1..50 range.

toolSearch.title: Tool Search
toolSearch.description: Compact large OpenClaw, MCP, and client tool catalogs behind one search/call surface. Set to true for the default code bridge or use the object form to choose the structured fallback.

enabled.title: Enable Code Mode
enabled.description: Enables generic code mode. Default is off. When explicitly enabled, OpenClaw fails closed if the runtime is unavailable instead of exposing the full tool list.

runtime.title: Code Mode Runtime
runtime.description: Guest JavaScript runtime. Only "quickjs-wasi" is supported.

mode.title: Code Mode Surface
mode.description: Model-facing surface. Only "only" is supported: expose code-mode `exec` and `wait` and hide normal tools.

languages.title: Code Mode Languages
languages.description: Accepted source languages for `exec`. Supported values are "javascript" and "typescript".

timeoutMs.title: Code Mode Timeout
timeoutMs.description: Maximum milliseconds for one code-mode `exec` or `wait` call.

memoryLimitBytes.title: Code Mode Memory Limit
memoryLimitBytes.description: QuickJS heap limit for one code-mode VM.

maxOutputBytes.title: Code Mode Output Limit
maxOutputBytes.description: Maximum serialized bytes returned through code-mode output.

maxSnapshotBytes.title: Code Mode Snapshot Limit
maxSnapshotBytes.description: Maximum serialized bytes retained for one suspended QuickJS snapshot.

maxPendingToolCalls.title: Code Mode Pending Tool Limit
maxPendingToolCalls.description: Maximum concurrent nested tool calls a code-mode VM can start before it must resume later.

snapshotTtlSeconds.title: Code Mode Snapshot TTL
snapshotTtlSeconds.description: How long suspended code-mode snapshots can be resumed with `wait` before they expire.

searchDefaultLimit.title: Code Mode Default Search Results
searchDefaultLimit.description: Default number of hidden catalog search results returned by `tools.search` inside code mode.

maxSearchLimit.title: Code Mode Max Search Results
maxSearchLimit.description: Maximum number of hidden catalog search results a code-mode program can request.

codeMode.title: Code Mode
codeMode.description: Generic OpenClaw code mode. When enabled, agent runs expose only `exec` and `wait` to the model and hide normal tools behind a QuickJS-WASI catalog bridge.

allowCrossContextSend.title: Allow Cross-Context Messaging
allowCrossContextSend.description: Legacy override: allow cross-context sends across all providers.

allowWithinProvider.title: Allow Cross-Context (Same Provider)
allowWithinProvider.description: Allow sends to other channels within the same provider (default: true).

allowAcrossProviders.title: Allow Cross-Context (Across Providers)
allowAcrossProviders.description: Allow sends across different providers (default: false).

enabled.title: Cross-Context Marker
enabled.description: Add a visible origin marker when sending cross-context (default: true).

allow.title: Message Action Allowlist
allow.description: Global message action allowlist for the message tool. Use only when the whole runtime should expose and accept a reduced action set; prefer per-agent allowlists for public or sandboxed agents.

enabled.title: Enable Message Broadcast
enabled.description: Enable broadcast action (default: true).

enabled.title: Enable Agent-to-Agent Tool
enabled.description: Enables the agent_to_agent tool surface so one agent can invoke another agent at runtime. Keep off in simple deployments and enable only when orchestration value outweighs complexity.

allow.title: Agent-to-Agent Target Allowlist
allow.description: Allowlist of target agent IDs permitted for agent_to_agent calls when orchestration is enabled. Use explicit allowlists to avoid uncontrolled cross-agent call graphs.

agentToAgent.title: Agent-to-Agent Tool Access
agentToAgent.description: Policy for allowing agent-to-agent tool calls and constraining which target agents can be reached. Keep disabled or tightly scoped unless cross-agent orchestration is intentionally enabled.

enabled.title: Enable Elevated Tool Access
enabled.description: Enables elevated tool execution path when sender and policy checks pass. Keep disabled in public/shared channels and enable only for trusted owner-operated contexts.

allowFrom.title: Elevated Tool Allow Rules
allowFrom.description: Sender allow rules for elevated tools, usually keyed by channel/provider identity formats. Use narrow, explicit identities so elevated commands cannot be triggered by unintended users.

elevated.title: Elevated Tool Access
elevated.description: Elevated tool access controls for privileged command surfaces that should only be reachable from trusted senders. Keep disabled unless operator workflows explicitly require elevated actions.

host.title: Exec Target
host.description: Selects execution target strategy for shell commands. Use "auto" for runtime-aware behavior (sandbox when available, otherwise gateway), or pin sandbox/gateway/node explicitly when you need a fixed surface.

mode.title: Exec Mode
mode.description: Normalized exec policy selector. Use "auto" for classifier-reviewed approval misses, "ask" for human-reviewed misses, "allowlist" for deterministic safe commands only, or "full" for trusted local operation.

security.title: Exec Security
security.description: Execution security posture selector controlling sandbox/approval expectations for command execution. Keep strict security mode for untrusted prompts and relax only for trusted operator workflows.

ask.title: Exec Ask
ask.description: Approval strategy for when exec commands require human confirmation before running. Use stricter ask behavior in shared channels and lower-friction settings in private operator contexts.

node.title: Exec Node Binding
node.description: Node binding configuration for exec tooling when command execution is delegated through connected nodes. Use explicit node binding only when multi-node routing is required.

pathPrepend.title: Exec PATH Prepend
pathPrepend.description: Directories to prepend to PATH for exec runs (gateway/sandbox).

safeBins.title: Exec Safe Bins
safeBins.description: Allow stdin-only safe binaries to run without explicit allowlist entries.

strictInlineEval.title: Require Inline-Eval Approval
strictInlineEval.description: Require explicit approval for interpreter inline-eval forms such as `python -c`, `node -e`, `ruby -e`, or `osascript -e`. Prevents silent allowlist reuse and downgrades allow-always to ask-each-time for those forms.

commandHighlighting.title: Exec Command Highlighting
commandHighlighting.description: Show parser-derived command highlights in exec approval prompts (default: false). Enable this to render highlighted command text without changing exec approval policy.

safeBinTrustedDirs.title: Exec Safe Bin Trusted Dirs
safeBinTrustedDirs.description: Additional explicit directories trusted for safe-bin path checks (PATH entries are never auto-trusted).

safeBinProfiles.title: Exec Safe Bin Profiles
safeBinProfiles.description: Optional per-binary safe-bin profiles (positional limits + allowed/denied flags).

model.title: Exec Reviewer Model
model.description: Optional provider/model override for the exec reviewer agent. Omit to reuse the current agent model.

timeoutMs.title: Exec Reviewer Timeout (ms)
timeoutMs.description: Exec reviewer timeout in milliseconds before falling back to human approval (default: 30000).

reviewer.title: Exec Reviewer
reviewer.description: Model-backed exec reviewer used by auto mode before human approval fallback. Configure a narrow model override here when you want exec review isolated from the main agent model.

notifyOnExit.title: Exec Notify On Exit
notifyOnExit.description: When true (default), backgrounded exec sessions on exit and node exec lifecycle events enqueue a system event and request a heartbeat.

notifyOnExitEmptySuccess.title: Exec Notify On Empty Success
notifyOnExitEmptySuccess.description: When true, successful backgrounded exec exits with empty output still enqueue a completion system event (default: false).

enabled.title: Enable apply_patch
enabled.description: Enable or disable apply_patch for OpenAI and OpenAI Codex models when allowed by tool policy (default: true).

workspaceOnly.title: apply_patch Workspace-Only
workspaceOnly.description: Restrict apply_patch paths to the workspace directory (default: true). Set false to allow writing outside the workspace (dangerous).

allowModels.title: apply_patch Model Allowlist
allowModels.description: Optional allowlist of model ids (e.g. "gpt-5.4" or "openai/gpt-5.4").

exec.title: Exec Tool
exec.description: Exec-tool policy grouping for shell execution host, security mode, approval behavior, and runtime bindings. Keep conservative defaults in production and tighten elevated execution paths.

workspaceOnly.title: Workspace-only FS tools
workspaceOnly.description: Restrict filesystem tools (read/write/edit/apply_patch) to the workspace directory (default: false).

tools.title: Subagent Tool Allow/Deny Policy
tools.description: Allow/deny tool policy applied to spawned subagent runtimes for per-subagent hardening. Keep this narrower than parent scope when subagents run semi-autonomous workflows.

subagents.title: Subagent Tool Policy
subagents.description: Tool policy wrapper for spawned subagents to restrict or expand tool availability compared to parent defaults. Use this to keep delegated agent capabilities scoped to task intent.

tools.title: Sandbox Tool Allow/Deny Policy
tools.description: Allow/deny tool policy applied when agents run in sandboxed execution environments. Keep policies minimal so sandbox tasks cannot escalate into unnecessary external actions.

sandbox.title: Sandbox Tool Policy
sandbox.description: Tool policy wrapper for sandboxed agent executions so sandbox runs can have distinct capability boundaries. Use this to enforce stronger safety in sandbox contexts.

planTool.title: Enable Structured Plan Tool
planTool.description: Enable the experimental structured `update_plan` tool for non-trivial multi-step work tracking. Leave this off unless you explicitly want the tool outside strict-agentic embedded OpenClaw runs.

experimental.title: Experimental Tools
experimental.description: Experimental built-in tool flags. Keep these off by default and enable only when you are intentionally testing a preview surface.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Global tool access policy and capability configuration across web, exec, media, messaging, and elevated surfaces. Use this section to constrain risky capabilities before broad rollout.

type.title: Binding Type
type.description: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.

agentId.title: Binding Agent ID
agentId.description: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.

channel.title: Binding Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.

accountId.title: Binding Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.

kind.title: Binding Peer Kind
kind.description: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.

id.title: Binding Peer ID
id.description: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.

peer.title: Binding Peer Match
peer.description: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.

guildId.title: Binding Guild ID
guildId.description: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.

teamId.title: Binding Team ID
teamId.description: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.

roles.title: Binding Roles
roles.description: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.

match.title: Binding Match Rule
match.description: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.

dmScope.title: Binding Session DM Scope
dmScope.description: Optional DM session scope override for this route binding. For example, keep global session.dmScope="main" while using "per-account-channel-peer" for selected direct peers.

session.title: Binding Session
session.description: Optional route session overrides for conversations matched by this binding. Use this when a narrow route should keep the same agent but isolate session continuity differently.

type.title: Binding Type
type.description: Binding kind. Use "route" (or omit for legacy route entries) for normal routing, and "acp" for persistent ACP conversation bindings.

agentId.title: Binding Agent ID
agentId.description: Target agent ID that receives traffic when the corresponding binding match rule is satisfied. Use valid configured agent IDs only so routing does not fail at runtime.

channel.title: Binding Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider identifier this binding applies to, such as `telegram`, `discord`, or a plugin channel ID. Use the configured channel key exactly so binding evaluation works reliably.

accountId.title: Binding Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups so the binding applies only to one identity. Use this when account scoping is required for the route and leave unset otherwise.

kind.title: Binding Peer Kind
kind.description: Peer conversation type: "direct", "group", "channel", or legacy "dm" (deprecated alias for direct). Prefer "direct" for new configs and keep kind aligned with channel semantics.

id.title: Binding Peer ID
id.description: Conversation identifier used with peer matching, such as a chat ID, channel ID, or group ID from the provider. Keep this exact to avoid silent non-matches.

peer.title: Binding Peer Match
peer.description: Optional peer matcher for specific conversations including peer kind and peer id. Use this when only one direct/group/channel target should be pinned to an agent.

guildId.title: Binding Guild ID
guildId.description: Optional Discord-style guild/server ID constraint for binding evaluation in multi-server deployments. Use this when the same peer identifiers can appear across different guilds.

teamId.title: Binding Team ID
teamId.description: Optional team/workspace ID constraint used by providers that scope chats under teams. Add this when you need bindings isolated to one workspace context.

roles.title: Binding Roles
roles.description: Optional role-based filter list used by providers that attach roles to chat context. Use this to route privileged or operational role traffic to specialized agents.

match.title: Binding Match Rule
match.description: Match rule object for deciding when a binding applies, including channel and optional account/peer constraints. Keep rules narrow to avoid accidental agent takeover across contexts.

mode.title: ACP Binding Mode
mode.description: ACP session mode override for this binding (persistent or oneshot).

label.title: ACP Binding Label
label.description: Human-friendly label for ACP status/diagnostics in this bound conversation.

cwd.title: ACP Binding Working Directory
cwd.description: Working directory override for ACP sessions created from this binding.

backend.title: ACP Binding Backend
backend.description: ACP backend override for this binding (falls back to agent runtime ACP backend, then global acp.backend).

acp.title: ACP Binding Overrides
acp.description: Optional per-binding ACP overrides for bindings[].type=acp. This layer overrides agents.list[].runtime.acp defaults for the matched conversation.

bindings.title: Bindings
bindings.description: Top-level binding rules for routing and persistent ACP conversation ownership. Use type=route for normal routing and type=acp for persistent ACP harness bindings.

strategy.title: Broadcast Strategy
strategy.description: Delivery order for broadcast fan-out: "parallel" sends to all targets concurrently, while "sequential" sends one-by-one. Use "parallel" for speed and "sequential" for stricter ordering/backpressure control.

additionalProperties.title: Broadcast Destination List
additionalProperties.description: Per-source broadcast destination list where each key is a source peer ID and the value is an array of destination peer IDs. Keep lists intentional to avoid accidental message amplification.

broadcast.title: Broadcast
broadcast.description: Broadcast routing map for sending the same outbound message to multiple peer IDs per source conversation. Keep this minimal and audited because one source can fan out to many destinations.

command.title: Audio Transcription Command
command.description: Executable + args used to transcribe audio (first token must be a safe binary/path), for example `["whisper-cli", "--model", "small", "{{MediaPath}}"]`. Deprecated `{input}` placeholders are migrated to `{{MediaPath}}` by `openclaw doctor --fix`.

timeoutSeconds.title: Audio Transcription Timeout (sec)
timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time allowed for the transcription command to finish before it is aborted. Increase this for longer recordings, and keep it tight in latency-sensitive deployments.

transcription.title: Audio Transcription
transcription.description: Command-based transcription settings for converting audio files into text before agent handling. Keep a simple, deterministic command path here so failures are easy to diagnose in logs.

audio.title: Audio
audio.description: Global audio ingestion settings used before higher-level tools process speech or media content. Configure this when you need deterministic transcription behavior for voice notes and clips.

preserveFilenames.title: Preserve Media Filenames
preserveFilenames.description: When enabled, uploaded media keeps its original filename instead of a generated temp-safe name. Turn this on when downstream automations depend on stable names, and leave off to reduce accidental filename leakage.

ttlHours.title: Media Retention TTL (hours)
ttlHours.description: Optional retention window in hours for persisted inbound media cleanup across the full media tree. Leave unset to preserve legacy behavior, or set values like 24 (1 day) or 168 (7 days) when you want automatic cleanup.

media.title: Media
media.description: Top-level media behavior shared across providers and tools that handle inbound files. Keep defaults unless you need stable filenames for external processing pipelines or longer-lived inbound media retention.

messagePrefix.title: Inbound Message Prefix
messagePrefix.description: Prefix text prepended to inbound user messages before they are handed to the agent runtime. Use this sparingly for channel context markers and keep it stable across sessions.

visibleReplies.title: Visible Replies
visibleReplies.description: Controls visible source replies across direct, group, and channel conversations. "message_tool" requires message(action=send) for visible output and keeps normal final text private. "automatic" posts normal replies as before.

responsePrefix.title: Outbound Response Prefix
responsePrefix.description: Prefix text prepended to outbound assistant replies before sending to channels. Use for lightweight branding/context tags and avoid long prefixes that reduce content density.

mentionPatterns.title: Group Mention Patterns
mentionPatterns.description: Safe case-insensitive regex patterns used to detect explicit mentions/trigger phrases in group chats. Use precise patterns to reduce false positives in high-volume channels; invalid or unsafe nested-repetition patterns are ignored.

historyLimit.title: Group History Limit
historyLimit.description: Maximum number of prior group messages loaded as context per turn for group sessions. Use higher values for richer continuity, or lower values for faster and cheaper responses.

unmentionedInbound.title: Group Unmentioned Inbound
unmentionedInbound.description: Controls how unmentioned always-on group chatter is submitted. "user_request" treats it as a user request; "room_event" submits it as quiet context where visible output requires the message tool.

visibleReplies.title: Group Visible Replies
visibleReplies.description: Overrides visible source replies for group/channel conversations. Defaults to "automatic" when no global visible reply policy is set. "message_tool" requires message(action=send) for room output and keeps normal final text private. "automatic" posts normal replies as before.

groupChat.title: Group Chat Rules
groupChat.description: Group-message handling controls including mention triggers and history window sizing. Keep mention patterns narrow so group channels do not trigger on every message.

mode.title: Queue Mode
mode.description: Queue mode for active runs. Use "steer" to inject prompts into the active run, "followup" to run later, "collect" to batch compatible messages later, or "interrupt" to abort the active run before starting the newest prompt.

byChannel.title: Queue Mode by Channel
byChannel.description: Per-channel queue mode overrides keyed by provider id (for example telegram, discord, slack). Use this when one channel's traffic pattern needs different behavior than global defaults.

debounceMs.title: Queue Fallback Debounce (ms)
debounceMs.description: Global fallback followup queue debounce window in milliseconds before draining buffered inbound messages. Default is 500ms; higher values coalesce bursts, lower values reduce latency.

debounceMsByChannel.title: Queue Fallback Debounce by Channel (ms)
debounceMsByChannel.description: Per-channel debounce overrides for queue behavior keyed by provider id. Use this to tune burst handling independently for chat surfaces with different pacing.

cap.title: Queue Capacity
cap.description: Maximum number of queued inbound items retained before drop policy applies. Default is 20; keep caps bounded in noisy channels so memory usage remains predictable.

drop.title: Queue Drop Strategy
drop.description: Drop strategy when queue cap is exceeded. "summarize" drops oldest entries but preserves compact summaries; "old" drops oldest without summaries; "new" rejects the newest item. Use "summarize" for long-running chats where context matters.

queue.title: Inbound Queue
queue.description: Queue strategy for inbound messages that arrive while a session run is active. Use this to tune steering, deferred followups, batching, or interruption.

debounceMs.title: Inbound Message Debounce (ms)
debounceMs.description: Debounce window (ms) for batching rapid inbound messages from the same sender (0 to disable).

byChannel.title: Inbound Debounce by Channel (ms)
byChannel.description: Per-channel inbound debounce overrides keyed by provider id in milliseconds. Use this where some providers send message fragments more aggressively than others.

inbound.title: Inbound Debounce
inbound.description: Direct inbound debounce settings used before queue/turn processing starts. Configure this for provider-specific rapid message bursts from the same sender.

ackReaction.title: Ack Reaction Emoji
ackReaction.description: Emoji reaction used to acknowledge inbound messages (empty disables).

ackReactionScope.title: Ack Reaction Scope
ackReactionScope.description: When to send ack reactions ("group-mentions", "group-all", "direct", "all", "off", "none"). "off"/"none" disables ack reactions entirely.

removeAckAfterReply.title: Remove Ack Reaction After Reply
removeAckAfterReply.description: Removes the acknowledgment reaction after final reply delivery when enabled. Keep enabled for cleaner UX in channels where persistent ack reactions create clutter.

enabled.title: Enable Status Reactions
enabled.description: Enable lifecycle status reactions on supported channels. Slack and Discord treat unset as enabled when ack reactions are active; Telegram and WhatsApp require this to be true before lifecycle reactions are used.

emojis.title: Status Reaction Emojis
emojis.description: Override default status reaction emojis. Keys: queued, thinking, compacting, tool, coding, web, deploy, build, concierge, done, error, stallSoft, stallHard. Telegram chooses the first supported fallback when a configured emoji is not available in the chat.

timing.title: Status Reaction Timing
timing.description: Override default timing. Keys: debounceMs (700), stallSoftMs (10000), stallHardMs (30000), doneHoldMs (1500), errorHoldMs (2500).

statusReactions.title: Status Reactions
statusReactions.description: Lifecycle status reactions that update the emoji on the trigger message as the agent progresses (queued → thinking → tool → done/error).

suppressToolErrors.title: Suppress Tool Error Warnings
suppressToolErrors.description: When true, suppress ⚠️ tool-error warnings from being shown to the user. The agent already sees errors in context and can retry. Default: false.

persona.title: TTS Persona
persona.description: Default TTS persona id. Local TTS persona preferences can override this per host.

prompt.title: TTS Persona Prompt
prompt.description: Provider-neutral persona prompt intent. Providers decide whether and how to map this into request instructions.

providers.title: TTS Persona Provider Bindings
providers.description: Provider-specific TTS persona bindings keyed by speech provider id. These merge over messages.tts.providers for the active persona.

additionalProperties.title: TTS Persona
additionalProperties.description: One TTS persona. Use provider-specific bindings for exact voices/models and prompt templates.

personas.title: TTS Personas
personas.description: Named TTS personas that define stable spoken identity plus provider-specific speech bindings.

apiKey.title: TTS Provider API Key
apiKey.description: Provider API key used by that speech provider when its plugin requires authenticated TTS access.

additionalProperties.title: TTS Provider Config
additionalProperties.description: Provider-specific TTS configuration for one speech provider id. Keep fields scoped to the plugin that owns that provider.

providers.title: TTS Provider Settings
providers.description: Provider-specific TTS settings keyed by speech provider id. Use this instead of bundled provider-specific top-level keys so speech plugins stay decoupled from core config schema.

tts.title: Message Text-to-Speech
tts.description: Text-to-speech policy for reading agent replies aloud on supported voice or audio surfaces. Keep disabled unless voice playback is part of your operator/user workflow.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Message formatting, acknowledgment, queueing, debounce, and status reaction behavior for inbound/outbound chat flows. Use this section when channel responsiveness or message UX needs adjustment.

native.title: Native Commands
native.description: Registers native slash/menu commands with channels that support command registration (Discord, Slack, Telegram). Keep enabled for discoverability unless you intentionally run text-only command workflows.

nativeSkills.title: Native Skill Commands
nativeSkills.description: Registers native skill commands so users can invoke skills directly from provider command menus where supported. Keep aligned with your skill policy so exposed commands match what operators expect.

text.title: Text Commands
text.description: Enables text-command parsing in chat input in addition to native command surfaces where available. Keep this enabled for compatibility across channels that do not support native command registration.

bash.title: Allow Bash Chat Command
bash.description: Allow bash chat command (`!`; `/bash` alias) to run host shell commands (default: false; requires tools.elevated).

bashForegroundMs.title: Bash Foreground Window (ms)
bashForegroundMs.description: How long bash waits before backgrounding (default: 2000; 0 backgrounds immediately).

config.title: Allow /config
config.description: Allow /config chat command to read/write config on disk (default: false).

mcp.title: Allow /mcp
mcp.description: Allow /mcp chat command to manage OpenClaw MCP server config under mcp.servers (default: false).

plugins.title: Allow /plugins
plugins.description: Allow /plugins chat command to list discovered plugins and toggle plugin enablement in config (default: false).

debug.title: Allow /debug
debug.description: Allow /debug chat command for runtime-only overrides (default: false).

restart.title: Allow Restart
restart.description: Allow /restart and gateway restart tool actions (default: true).

ownerAllowFrom.title: Command Owners
ownerAllowFrom.description: Explicit owner allowlist for owner-scoped commands. Use channel-native IDs (optionally prefixed like "whatsapp:+15551234567"). '*' is ignored.

ownerDisplay.title: Owner ID Display
ownerDisplay.description: Controls how owner IDs are rendered in the system prompt. Allowed values: raw, hash. Default: raw.

ownerDisplaySecret.title: Owner ID Hash Secret
ownerDisplaySecret.description: Optional secret used to HMAC hash owner IDs when ownerDisplay=hash. Prefer env substitution.

allowFrom.title: Command Elevated Access Rules
allowFrom.description: Defines elevated command allow rules by channel and sender for owner-level command surfaces. Use narrow provider-specific identities so privileged commands are not exposed to broad chat audiences.

commands.title: Commands
commands.description: Controls chat command surfaces, owner gating, and elevated command access behavior across providers. Keep defaults unless you need stricter operator controls or broader command availability.

enabled.title: Forward Exec Approvals
enabled.description: Enables forwarding of exec approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Keep disabled in low-risk setups and enable only when human approval responders need channel-visible prompts.

mode.title: Approval Forwarding Mode
mode.description: Controls where approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths. Use "session" as baseline and expand only when operational workflow requires redundancy.

agentFilter.title: Approval Agent Filter
agentFilter.description: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius and avoid notifying channels for unrelated agents.

sessionFilter.title: Approval Session Filter
sessionFilter.description: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded to shared destinations.

channel.title: Approval Target Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id. Use valid channel IDs only so approvals do not silently fail due to unknown routes.

to.title: Approval Target Destination
to.description: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider). Verify semantics per provider because destination format differs across channel integrations.

accountId.title: Approval Target Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when approvals must route through a specific account context. Use this only when the target channel has multiple configured identities.

threadId.title: Approval Target Thread ID
threadId.description: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded approvals. Use this to keep approval traffic contained in operational threads instead of main channels.

targets.title: Approval Forwarding Targets
targets.description: Explicit delivery targets used when forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details. Keep target lists least-privilege and validate each destination before enabling broad forwarding.

exec.title: Exec Approval Forwarding
exec.description: Groups exec-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Configure here when approval prompts must reach operational channels instead of only the origin thread.

enabled.title: Forward Plugin Approvals
enabled.description: Enables forwarding of plugin approval requests to configured delivery destinations (default: false). Independent of approvals.exec.enabled.

mode.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding Mode
mode.description: Controls where plugin approval prompts are sent: "session" uses origin chat, "targets" uses configured targets, and "both" sends to both paths.

agentFilter.title: Plugin Approval Agent Filter
agentFilter.description: Optional allowlist of agent IDs eligible for forwarded plugin approvals, for example `["primary", "ops-agent"]`. Use this to limit forwarding blast radius.

sessionFilter.title: Plugin Approval Session Filter
sessionFilter.description: Optional session-key filters matched as substring or regex-style patterns, for example `["discord:", "^agent:ops:"]`. Use narrow patterns so only intended approval contexts are forwarded.

channel.title: Plugin Approval Target Channel
channel.description: Channel/provider ID used for forwarded plugin approval delivery, such as discord, slack, or a plugin channel id.

to.title: Plugin Approval Target Destination
to.description: Destination identifier inside the target channel (channel ID, user ID, or thread root depending on provider).

accountId.title: Plugin Approval Target Account ID
accountId.description: Optional account selector for multi-account channel setups when plugin approvals must route through a specific account context.

threadId.title: Plugin Approval Target Thread ID
threadId.description: Optional thread/topic target for channels that support threaded delivery of forwarded plugin approvals.

targets.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding Targets
targets.description: Explicit delivery targets used when plugin approval forwarding mode includes targets, each with channel and destination details.

plugin.title: Plugin Approval Forwarding
plugin.description: Groups plugin-approval forwarding behavior including enablement, routing mode, filters, and explicit targets. Independent of exec approval forwarding. Configure here when plugin approval prompts must reach operational channels.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.description: Approval routing controls for forwarding exec and plugin approval requests to chat destinations outside the originating session. Keep these disabled unless operators need explicit out-of-band approval visibility.

scope.title: Session Scope
scope.description: Sets base session grouping strategy: "per-sender" isolates by sender and "global" shares one session per channel context. Keep "per-sender" for safer multi-user behavior unless deliberate shared context is required.

dmScope.title: DM Session Scope
dmScope.description: DM session scoping: "main" keeps continuity, while "per-peer", "per-channel-peer", and "per-account-channel-peer" increase isolation. Use isolated modes for shared inboxes or multi-account deployments.

identityLinks.title: Session Identity Links
identityLinks.description: Maps canonical identities to provider-prefixed peer IDs so equivalent users resolve to one DM thread (example: telegram:123456). Use this when the same human appears across multiple channels or accounts.

resetTriggers.title: Session Reset Triggers
resetTriggers.description: Lists message triggers that force a session reset when matched in inbound content. Use sparingly for explicit reset phrases so context is not dropped unexpectedly during normal conversation.

idleMinutes.title: Session Idle Minutes
idleMinutes.description: Applies a legacy idle reset window in minutes for session reuse behavior across inactivity gaps. Use this only for compatibility and prefer structured reset policies under session.reset/session.resetByType.

mode.title: Session Reset Mode
mode.description: Selects reset strategy: "daily" resets at a configured hour and "idle" resets after inactivity windows. Keep one clear mode per policy to avoid surprising context turnover patterns.

atHour.title: Session Daily Reset Hour
atHour.description: Sets local-hour boundary (0-23) for daily reset mode so sessions roll over at predictable times. Use with mode=daily and align to operator timezone expectations for human-readable behavior.

idleMinutes.title: Session Reset Idle Minutes
idleMinutes.description: Sets inactivity window before reset for idle mode and can also act as secondary guard with daily mode. Use larger values to preserve continuity or smaller values for fresher short-lived threads.

reset.title: Session Reset Policy
reset.description: Defines the default reset policy object used when no type-specific or channel-specific override applies. Set this first, then layer resetByType or resetByChannel only where behavior must differ.

direct.title: Session Reset (Direct)
direct.description: Defines reset policy for direct chats and supersedes the base session.reset configuration for that type. Use this as the canonical direct-message override instead of the legacy dm alias.

dm.title: Session Reset (DM Deprecated Alias)
dm.description: Deprecated alias for direct reset behavior kept for backward compatibility with older configs. Use session.resetByType.direct instead so future tooling and validation remain consistent.

group.title: Session Reset (Group)
group.description: Defines reset policy for group chat sessions where continuity and noise patterns differ from DMs. Use shorter idle windows for busy groups if context drift becomes a problem.

thread.title: Session Reset (Thread)
thread.description: Defines reset policy for thread-scoped sessions, including focused channel thread workflows. Use this when thread sessions should expire faster or slower than other chat types.

resetByType.title: Session Reset by Chat Type
resetByType.description: Overrides reset behavior by chat type (direct, group, thread) when defaults are not sufficient. Use this when group/thread traffic needs different reset cadence than direct messages.

resetByChannel.title: Session Reset by Channel
resetByChannel.description: Provides channel-specific reset overrides keyed by provider/channel id for fine-grained behavior control. Use this only when one channel needs exceptional reset behavior beyond type-level policies.

store.title: Session Store Path
store.description: Sets the session storage file path used to persist session records across restarts. Use an explicit path only when you need custom disk layout, backup routing, or mounted-volume storage.

typingIntervalSeconds.title: Session Typing Interval (seconds)
typingIntervalSeconds.description: Controls interval for repeated typing indicators while replies are being prepared in typing-capable channels. Increase to reduce chatty updates or decrease for more active typing feedback.

typingMode.title: Session Typing Mode
typingMode.description: Controls typing behavior timing: "never", "instant", "thinking", or "message" based emission points. Keep conservative modes in high-volume channels to avoid unnecessary typing noise.

mainKey.title: Session Main Key
mainKey.description: Overrides the canonical main session key used for continuity when dmScope or routing logic points to "main". Use a stable value only if you intentionally need custom session anchoring.

default.title: Session Send Policy Default Action
default.description: Sets fallback action when no sendPolicy rule matches: "allow" or "deny". Keep "allow" for simpler setups, or choose "deny" when you require explicit allow rules for every destination.

action.title: Session Send Rule Action
action.description: Defines rule decision as "allow" or "deny" when the corresponding match criteria are satisfied. Use deny-first ordering when enforcing strict boundaries with explicit allow exceptions.

channel.title: Session Send Rule Channel
channel.description: Matches rule application to a specific channel/provider id (for example discord, telegram, slack). Use this when one channel should permit or deny delivery independently of others.

chatType.title: Session Send Rule Chat Type
chatType.description: Matches rule application to chat type (direct, group, thread) so behavior varies by conversation form. Use this when DM and group destinations require different safety boundaries.

keyPrefix.title: Session Send Rule Key Prefix
keyPrefix.description: Matches a normalized session-key prefix after internal key normalization steps in policy consumers. Use this for general prefix controls, and prefer rawKeyPrefix when exact full-key matching is required.

rawKeyPrefix.title: Session Send Rule Raw Key Prefix
rawKeyPrefix.description: Matches the raw, unnormalized session-key prefix for exact full-key policy targeting. Use this when normalized keyPrefix is too broad and you need agent-prefixed or transport-specific precision.

match.title: Session Send Rule Match
match.description: Defines optional rule match conditions that can combine channel, chatType, and key-prefix constraints. Keep matches narrow so policy intent stays readable and debugging remains straightforward.

rules.title: Session Send Policy Rules
rules.description: Ordered allow/deny rules evaluated before the default action, for example `{ action: "deny", match: { channel: "discord" } }`. Put most specific rules first so broad rules do not shadow exceptions.

sendPolicy.title: Session Send Policy
sendPolicy.description: Controls cross-session send permissions using allow/deny rules evaluated against channel, chatType, and key prefixes. Use this to fence where session tools can deliver messages in complex environments.

acquireTimeoutMs.title: Session Write Lock Acquire Timeout
acquireTimeoutMs.description: Milliseconds to wait while acquiring a session transcript write lock before reporting the session as busy. Default: 60000; env override: OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS.

staleMs.title: Session Write Lock Stale Timeout
staleMs.description: Milliseconds before an existing session transcript lock can be treated as stale and reclaimed. Default: 1800000; env override: OPENCLAW_SESSION_WRITE_LOCK_STALE_MS.

maxHoldMs.title: Session Write Lock Max Hold
